volumeTitle: Stichus. Trinummus. Truculentus. Tale of a Travelling Bag. Fragments
label: Truculentus (Section 4.3.785 - Section 4.3.845)

sections.label: Section 4.3.785
sections.original: Etiamnum quid sit negoti , falsus incertusque sum ,
sections.translation: Even now , what ’ s the matter , I ’ m at a loss to know and uncertain ; except that still I ’ m afraid . [*]

sections.label: Section 4.3.786
sections.original: nisi quia timeo tamen , egomet quia quod peccavi scio .
sections.translation: What I ’ ve done wrong I know not .

sections.label: Section 4.3.787
sections.original: Omnium primum diversae state — em sic , istuc volo ;
sections.translation: First of all , then , you stand apart . ( They stand apart .) Aye , so ; that ’ s what I mean ; that you mayn ’ t be making signs between you , I ’ ll be a party-wall . ( To his MAID-SERVANT .) Speak you .

sections.label: Section 4.3.789
sections.original: Quid loquar ?
sections.translation: What am I to speak about ?

sections.label: Section 4.3.789b
sections.original: Quid puero factumst , mea quem peperit filia ,
sections.translation: What was done with the child that my daughter was delivered of ? My grandchild , I mean ? Tell me the circumstances of the case .

sections.label: Section 4.3.790b
sections.original: Istae dedi .
sections.translation: I gave it to her .

sections.label: Section 4.3.791
sections.original: Iam tace . accepistin puerum tú ab hac ?
sections.translation: Now hold your tongue . ( To SYRA .) Did you receive the child from her ?

sections.label: Section 4.3.791b
sections.original: Accepi .
sections.translation: I did receive it .

sections.label: Section 4.3.791c
sections.original: Tace .
sections.translation: Hold your tongue ; I want no more ; you ’ ve confessed enough .

sections.label: Section 4.3.792b
sections.original: Infitias non eo .
sections.translation: I ’ m not going to deny it .

sections.label: Section 4.3.793
sections.original: Iam livorem verbo scapulis istoc concinnas tuis .
sections.translation: By this you ’ ve now caused some relief for your shoulder-blades . So far , the account of each of them tallies .

sections.label: Section 4.3.794b
sections.original: Vae misero mihi ,
sections.translation: Ah wretched me !

sections.label: Section 4.3.795
sections.original: mea nunc facinora aperiuntur , clam quae speravi fore .
sections.translation: my doings are now being disclosed , which I hoped would be concealed .

sections.label: Section 4.3.796
sections.original: Loquere tu . qui dare te huic puerum iussit ?
sections.translation: Speak , you . Who bade you give the child to her ?

sections.label: Section 4.3.796b
sections.original: Era maior mea .
sections.translation: My elder mistress .

sections.label: Section 4.3.797
sections.original: Quid tu , cur eum accepisti ?
sections.translation: What say you ? Why did you receive it ?

sections.label: Section 4.3.797b
sections.original: Era med oravit minor ,
sections.translation: My young mistress entreated me that the child might be brought , and that all this might remain secret .

sections.label: Section 4.3.799
sections.original: Loquere tu . quid eo fecisti puero ?
sections.translation: Speak , you . What did you do with this child ?

sections.label: Section 4.3.799b
sections.original: Ad meam eram detuli .
sections.translation: I took it to my mistress .

sections.label: Section 4.3.800
sections.original: Quid eo puero tua era fecit ?
sections.translation: What did your mistress do with this child ?

sections.label: Section 4.3.800b
sections.original: Erae meae extemplo dedit .
sections.translation: Gave it at once to my mistress .

sections.label: Section 4.3.801
sections.original: Quoi , malum , erae ?
sections.translation: Plague on it , to what mistress ?

sections.label: Section 4.3.801b
sections.original: Duae sunt istae .
sections.translation: There are two of them .

sections.label: Section 4.3.801c
sections.original: Cave tu nisi quod te rogo .
sections.translation: Take you care , unless I ask you anything , only to answer that which I ask of you .

sections.label: Section 4.3.802b
sections.original: Mater , inquam , filiae dono dedit .
sections.translation: The mother , I say , made a present of it to the daughter .

sections.label: Section 4.3.803
sections.original: Plus quam dudum inqua loquere .
sections.translation: You are saying more than you did just now .

sections.label: Section 4.3.803b
sections.original: Plus tu rogitas .
sections.translation: You are asking more .

sections.label: Section 4.3.803c
sections.original: Responde ocius :
sections.translation: Answer me quickly ; what did she do , to whom it was given ? Tell me .

sections.label: Section 4.3.804b
sections.original: Supposivit .
sections.translation: She passed it off as —

sections.label: Section 4.3.804c
sections.original: Cui ?
sections.translation: Whose ?

sections.label: Section 4.3.804d
sections.original: Sibi .
sections.translation: As her own

sections.label: Section 4.3.805
sections.original: Pro filiolon ?
sections.translation: son .

sections.label: Section 4.3.805b
sections.original: Pro filiolo .
sections.translation: As her own son ?

sections.label: Section 4.3.805c
sections.original: Di , obsecro vostram fidem ,
sections.translation: Ye Gods , by my trust in you I do appeal to you , how much more easily does another than she to whom it belongs , bring forth another ’ s child ! She , by the labours of another , has brought forth this child without pain . A child blest indeed ! two mothers it has got , and grandams two [*] . I ’ m now afraid how many fathers there may have been . Do see , please , the shocking deeds of women !

sections.label: Section 4.3.810
sections.original: Magis pol haec malitia pertinét ad viros quam ad mulieres :
sections.translation: I ’ troth , this fraud relates rather to the men than to the women . ’ Twas a man , and not a woman , that caused her pregnancy .

sections.label: Section 4.3.811b
sections.original: Idem ego istuc scio .
sections.translation: I know that too . You were a trusty guardian for it .

sections.label: Section 4.3.812b
sections.original: Plus potest qui plus valet .
sections.translation: He can do the most , who is strong the most . He was a man ; he was the strongest ; he prevailed ; what he wanted , he carried off .

sections.label: Section 4.3.814
sections.original: Et tibi quidem hercle ídem attulit magnum malum .
sections.translation: And , i ’ faith , he too brought a heavy mishap , in fact , upon yourself .

sections.label: Section 4.3.815
sections.original: Idem istuc ipsa , etsi tu taceas , reapse experta intellego .
sections.translation: The thing itself experienced , I myself fully know that , even if you had held your tongue .

sections.label: Section 4.3.816
sections.original: Numquam te facere hodie quivi , ut is quis esset diceres .
sections.translation: Never , this day , have I been able to made you declare who he was .

sections.label: Section 4.3.817
sections.original: Tacui adhuc : nunc non tacebo , quando adest nec se indicat .
sections.translation: I ’ ve held my tongue ; but now I shan ’ t hold my tongue , since he ’ s here ; it ’ s necessary I should tell .

sections.label: Section 4.3.818
sections.original: Lapideus sum , commovere me miser non audeo .
sections.translation: I ’ m petrified ; in my wretchedness , I dare not move myself ; the matter ’ s all out ! The trial ’ s now going on here for my life !

sections.label: Section 4.3.820
sections.original: meum illuc facinus , mea stultitia est . timeo , quam mox nominer .
sections.translation: These are my misdeeds , this is my folly . I ’ m in dread how soon I may be named .

sections.label: Section 4.3.821
sections.original: Loquere , filiam meam quis integram stupraverit .
sections.translation: Speak out , who was it , debauched my maiden daughter ?

sections.label: Section 4.3.822
sections.original: Video ego té , propter male facta qui es patronus parieti .
sections.translation: I see him near you . A supporter of the wall [*] .

sections.label: Section 4.3.823
sections.original: Neque vivos neque mortuos sum , ne que quid nunc faciam scio ,
sections.translation: I ’ m neither alive nor dead , nor know I what I am now to do ; neither know I how to go away hence , nor how to accost him ; I ’ m numbed with fear .

sections.label: Section 4.3.825
sections.original: Dicin an non ?
sections.translation: Will you tell me , or no ?

sections.label: Section 4.3.825b
sections.original: Diniarchus , quoi illam prius desponderas .
sections.translation: It is Dinarchus , to whom you first betrothed her .

sections.label: Section 4.3.826
sections.original: Vbi is homost quem dicis ?
sections.translation: Where is this person whom you mention ?

sections.label: Section 4.3.826b
sections.original: Adsum , Callicles . per tua obsecro
sections.translation: Here I am , Callicles . ( Falling on the ground .) By your knees I do entreat you that you will bear with wisdom that which was done in folly ; and that you will pardon me that , which , losing my senses , I did through the bad influence of wine .

sections.label: Section 4.3.829
sections.original: Non placet qui in mutum culpam confert , qui non quit loqui .
sections.translation: You please me not . You throw the blame on what is dumb [*] , that which cannot speak .

sections.label: Section 4.3.830
sections.original: nam vinum si fabulari possit , se defenderet .
sections.translation: But the wine , if it could speak , would defend itself . It ’ s not wine that ’ s in the habit of ruling men , but men wine ; those , indeed , who are virtuous men ; but he who is bad , although he drinks water , or if indeed he abstains from intoxicating liquors , still , by nature he ’ s bad .

sections.label: Section 4.3.834
sections.original: Scio equidem quae nolo multa mi audienda ob noxiam .
sections.translation: Well , I ’ m sensible that many reproaches must be heard by me , which I would prefer not .

sections.label: Section 4.3.835
sections.original: ego tibi me obnoxium esse fateor culpae compotem .
sections.translation: I confess that I ’ ve offended you , and am privy to the crime .

sections.label: Section 4.3.836
sections.original: Callicles , vide in quaéstione ne facias iniuriam :
sections.translation: Callicles , prithee beware that you do injury to no person ; the accused is pleading his cause at large , the witnesses you are keeping in bonds .

sections.label: Section 4.3.838
sections.original: Solvite istas . agite , abite tu domum et tu autem domum .
sections.translation: Release those women . ( They are unbound .) Come ( to each of them in turn ) , do you be off home , and you home as well . ( To SYRA .) Tell your mistress this : she must give up the child , if any one asks for it . ( SYRA goes into the house of PHRONESIUM , and exit the MAID-SERVANT .)

sections.label: Section 4.3.840
sections.original: eamus tu in ius .
sections.translation: You , Dinarchus , let ’ s go before the judge .

sections.label: Section 4.3.840b
sections.original: Quid vis in ius me ire ? tu es praetor mihi .
sections.translation: Why do you wish me to go before the judge ? You are the Praetor to me . But I entreat of you , Callicles , that you ’ ll give me your daughter for a wife .

sections.label: Section 4.3.842
sections.original: Eundem pol te iudicasse qui admisti eam rem intellego .
sections.translation: I ’ faith , I find , indeed , that you ’ ve come to a decision on that point yourself ; for you haven ’ t waited till I gave her ; you have helped yourself . Now keep her , as you ’ ve got her , but I ’ ll fine you this grand haul ;

sections.label: Section 4.3.845
sections.original: sex talenta magna dotis demam pro ista inscitia .
sections.translation: six great talents will I deduct from her dowry for this folly .
